hard.next_step: Per the conversation with Dwayne Nicolay, they are open to evaluating Zycus and a meeting has been set for 26th June 2026. They are purely an in-house systems and no proper structure. They are completely running on manual ERP and are looking at CLM and S2C as priority.
hard.ais_why: Cox Automotive has a strong digital transformation and innovation culture, with significant investments in AI, data analytics, automation, and connected automotive solutions. Its procurement organization is likely focused on improving sourcing efficiency, supplier collaboration, contract management, spend visibility, and risk management through AI. Given the company's enterprise scale and technology-first approach, AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P), Intake Management, and autonomous procurement capabilities align well with its operational and strategic objectives. Cox Automotive is a strong candidate for advanced procurement AI initiatives.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage qualified greenfield deal moving on pace: the S2C/CLM demo is now locked for 4 Aug 2026 after Dwayne Nicolay confirmed the slot ('That works', 26 Jun 2026). Discovery ran 25 Jun 2026, the buyer is responsive, and the 31 Dec 2026 close (178 days out) is credible for a Qualified-stage enterprise motion. The open work is depth, not pace: no budget owner is mapped and the only real threat is do-nothing inertia.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 17 Jun 2026, qualified 25 Jun 2026 (current stage), ~11 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul 2026. 178 days to close (31 Dec 2026). Discovery 25 Jun, buyer confirmed demo date 26 Jun, demo locked 4 Aug 2026 (~4 weeks out). No slip recorded since creation. A 12-15 month enterprise motion supports a Dec close only if the demo converts to RFI/RFP and vendor selection by Q3 2026; single-thread and unmapped budget authority are the pacing risks, not the date itself.
evidence: Demo confirmed for 4 Aug 2026: Dwayne Nicolay replied 'That works' on 26 Jun 2026 to Luke Dougherty's proposed Tuesday 4 Aug 10am 90-minute slot (SF email tasks, 26 Jun 2026)
Discovery completed 25 Jun 2026; buyer engaged and responsive across three same-week email exchanges (SF activities, 25-26 Jun 2026)
Pain confirmed: Cox is 'purely in-house systems and no proper structure', 'completely running on manual ERP', prioritising CLM and S2C (SF Next_Step__c + 25 Jun 2026 discovery)
Products in scope: iContract, iSource, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake, a $200k multi-product greenfield build (SF Products)
AIS 9 / Strong signals genuine AI appetite that manual ERP cannot serve (SF AIS_Score__c / AIS_Status__c)
No external vendor named; the live threat is do-nothing inertia, Cox having reopened procurement only ~1 year ago post-COVID (25 Jun 2026 discovery)
Single-threaded on Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer); no economic buyer or budget owner mapped for the $200k spend (SF OpportunityContactRole)

metrics.narrative: Cox has articulated clear qualitative pain but no quantified value case. Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager) described Cox as 'purely in-house systems and no proper structure', 'completely running on manual ERP', with CLM and S2C 'as priority' (SF Next_Step__c; 25 Jun 2026 discovery). No savings target, cycle-time, error-rate, headcount, or compliance figure has been named, and no forcing event (audit, contract expiry, regulatory mandate) exists. Because this is a $200k greenfield build that will need senior budget sign-off, quantifying the cost of the current manual process and the ROI of automation is the single highest-value next step and should anchor the 4 Aug 2026 demo.
sources: SF Next_Step__c: 'They are purely an in-house systems and no proper structure. They are completely running on manual ERP and are looking at CLM and S2C as priority.'
Discovery call 25 Jun 2026 (Dwayne Nicolay): manual ERP, no structure, CLM+S2C priority

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The only Salesforce contact role on the opportunity is Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer, primary), who owns the procurement rebuild but does not hold the budget authority a $200k enterprise commitment requires. Nothing in the emails or Next Step names who signs, and no COO/CFO/finance stakeholder has appeared on any activity. The evidence does show budget authority sits above Dwayne, since Cox reopened the procurement function centrally post-COVID, so identifying and engaging that owner, ideally by having Dwayne bring them to the 4 Aug 2026 demo, is a ranked move; naming them is not something to invent.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: only Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer, primary)
SF Next_Step__c and 25 Jun 2026 discovery: no budget owner named; $200k multi-product scope

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are early and inferred from the discovery, not yet formally documented. Dwayne Nicolay framed the priority as CLM and S2C to replace manual ERP and give Cox 'proper structure', so the implicit criteria are contract lifecycle management, structured sourcing, and a scalable foundation for a greenfield procurement function (25 Jun 2026 discovery). His interest in spend intelligence and supplier discovery, plus the Strong AIS signal, points to AI capability (Merlin / Merlin Intake) being a real evaluation axis. Formal decision criteria, weightings, and any RFI/RFP structure are still to be established; the 4 Aug 2026 demo is the venue to confirm and prioritise them with the buyer.
sources: Discovery call 25 Jun 2026 (Dwayne Nicolay): CLM + S2C priority, wants structure over manual ERP
SF Products: iContract, iSource, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake; AIS 9 / Strong

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is not yet visible. Only two events sit on the calendar: the 25 Jun 2026 discovery and the confirmed 4 Aug 2026 demo, both with Dwayne Nicolay as the sole Cox participant, so no committee, approval chain, or procurement path has surfaced. There is no stated timeline for vendor selection, no named approvers, and no procurement/legal channel identified. Because Cox is rebuilding procurement from scratch, the process is likely being defined as they go; establishing who else must be involved and what the internal path to a $200k approval looks like is a priority to protect the Dec close date.
sources: SF activities: 25 Jun 2026 discovery and 4 Aug 2026 demo, Dwayne Nicolay only
SF OpportunityContactRole: single contact, no approval chain documented

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view yet, which is normal for a Qualified-stage new-business deal this early. There is no MSA/SOW discussion, no procurement or legal contact, no InfoSec trigger, and no current-contract expiry to act as a forcing function (Cox is greenfield with no incumbent to replace). Visibility here will come naturally once the demo converts to a selection; the near-term move is not paper but to establish the evaluation-to-decision path so the eventual contracting phase has a runway inside the 31 Dec 2026 target.
sources: SF: no contract, legal, or InfoSec activity logged; no Current_Contract_Expiration on record
25 Jun 2026 discovery: greenfield, 'no proper structure', no incumbent

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and the owner is clear. Cox runs 'completely manual ERP' with 'no proper structure' for CLM or S2C and is rebuilding procurement from scratch after closing the function during COVID and reopening it ~1 year ago (25 Jun 2026 discovery, Dwayne Nicolay; SF Next_Step__c). The pain is operational: no centralised contract repository, no structured sourcing, no supplier management, ad-hoc manual workflows. Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager) owns the pain directly, having taken the role to professionalise the function, with budget escalation implied above him. The gap is that the cost of this pain has not been quantified, which is what would convert acknowledged pain into urgency.
sources: Discovery call 25 Jun 2026 (Dwayne Nicolay): manual ERP, no structure, procurement rebuilt post-COVID
SF Next_Step__c: manual ERP, no structure, CLM and S2C as priority

champion.narrative: Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer) is a developing, not yet confirmed, champion. He initiated and drives the evaluation: he took the discovery on 25 Jun 2026, replied same-week to lock the demo ('That works', 26 Jun 2026), and personally owns the procurement rebuild, all signs of genuine ownership. What is still missing is advocacy behaviour that defines a true champion: he has not multi-threaded, has not introduced or named a budget owner, and has not voiced a preference for Zycus over doing nothing. His authority is also limited, an Influencer without budget control. The 4 Aug 2026 demo is the test: does he bring other stakeholders, share internal timelines, and start selling internally.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer, primary)
SF email tasks 25-26 Jun 2026: Dwayne responsive, confirmed demo date, follow-through
Gap: no multi-threading, no budget-owner intro, no stated Zycus preference

competition.narrative: There is no external vendor competitor; the real contest is against do-nothing inertia. Cox is greenfield, 'purely in-house systems, no proper structure' on 'completely manual ERP', so there is no incumbent platform to displace and no rival (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP, Zip) has been named in any field or on any call. The risk is that a function only reopened ~1 year ago, with no forcing event, deprioritises or delays. The wedge is to make the cost of manual processes explicit and position Zycus CLM + S2C as the structured foundation Cox needs, with Merlin / Merlin Intake as differentiated AI capability manual ERP cannot match.
sources: 25 Jun 2026 discovery (Dwayne Nicolay): greenfield, manual ERP, no incumbent
SF Competitors__c blank; no rival named in any field, activity, or Next Step

deal_movement.summary: Since qualification on 25 Jun 2026 the deal has advanced from an initial discovery to a confirmed evaluation: the buyer locked the S2C/CLM demo for 4 Aug 2026. Momentum is steady and rep-driven; no negative movement, but no new stakeholders or commercial signal have entered.

items.change: Demo confirmed for 4 Aug 2026 after Dwayne Nicolay replied 'That works' to Luke Dougherty's proposed slot
Discovery deck and monthly eBook sent to Dwayne Nicolay following the discovery call
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified; owner reassigned to Luke Dougherty

competitive_position.summary: No external vendor is competing; the single current threat is do-nothing / status-quo inertia. Cox shut procurement during COVID and reopened it only ~1 year ago (25 Jun 2026 discovery, Dwayne Nicolay), runs 'completely manual ERP', and has no forcing event (contract expiry, audit, mandate) to compel a decision. With no rival to beat, the win depends on manufacturing urgency: a quantified cost-of-inaction case and a Merlin-led AI story that manual ERP cannot match, landed at the 4 Aug 2026 demo.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo Inertia
competitors.quote: They are purely an in-house systems and no proper structure. They are completely running on manual ERP and are looking at CLM and S2C as priority.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of manual procurement (cycle time, error rate, compliance and capacity waste) and position Zycus CLM + S2C as the structured foundation Cox needs post-COVID, with Merlin / Merlin Intake as the differentiated AI layer; co-create a mutual evaluation timeline with internal milestones to beat inertia.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Cox's stated priorities (CLM and structured S2C to replace manual ERP) map directly onto the in-scope Zycus modules, so fit is strong on paper; the exposure is that expectations for AI value and for a phased, affordable rollout have not yet been tested against a demo.

Cox Automotive is a strong candidate for advanced procurement AI initiatives

manual ERP pain stated but no cost, cycle-time, or ROI figure named
single contact across discovery and the confirmed demo

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers raise win probability now: get a budget owner into the room, arm the demo with a quantified cost-of-inaction case, and lead with the Merlin AI story that differentiates from manual ERP.
flags: Break single-threading before 4 Aug: Dwayne Nicolay is an Influencer with no budget authority, so ask him to bring the procurement budget owner to the demo.
Beat do-nothing inertia with numbers: build a quantified cost-of-manual-process and ROI case, since there is no forcing event to compel a $200k decision.
Lead the 4 Aug demo with Merlin / Merlin Intake: AIS 9 (Strong) signals AI appetite that manual ERP cannot serve, and it is the clearest differentiator.

items.name: Dwayne Nicolay
items.title: Procurement Manager
items.risk: Single-threaded and an Influencer without budget authority; the deal is exposed if he deprioritises or if a senior owner is never engaged. Developing champion, advocacy not yet proven.
items.sentiment: Positive: initiated the evaluation, engaged in discovery, and confirmed the 4 Aug demo same week; owns the procurement rebuild.

champion_strength.summary: Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer) is a developing champion: he initiated the evaluation, owns the procurement rebuild, and drove the demo booking ('That works', 26 Jun 2026), but he has not multi-threaded, has not introduced a budget owner, and has not stated a Zycus preference. His limited authority means securing a higher-power sponsor is the next champion-building step; the 4 Aug 2026 demo is where his advocacy will show.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope and Cox shows Strong AI appetite (AIS 9), but the agentic/AI story has not yet been put in front of the buyer; it should lead the 4 Aug 2026 demo as the key differentiator versus manual ERP.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c is 9 with AIS_Status__c 'Strong': Cox is a strong candidate for procurement AI given its digital-transformation culture. No buyer call this run to over/under-rate the field, but Merlin and Merlin Intake being in scope aligns with the signal.

items.detail: No economic buyer or budget owner mapped for a $200k multi-product commitment; approval authority sits above Dwayne Nicolay and is unengaged.
Greenfield function rebuilt only ~1 year ago post-COVID with no forcing event; do-nothing inertia is the primary loss risk.
Single-threaded on one Influencer; a senior stakeholder could deprioritise the project without our visibility.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and rep-driven: the buyer confirmed the next step (4 Aug demo) within a day, and there is no cooling. Depth is thin (one contact, no quantified case), which caps confidence rather than signalling decline.
items: Buyer confirmed 4 Aug 2026 demo same week (26 Jun 2026)
No stakeholder breadth or commercial signal yet

critical_signals.text: Dwayne Nicolay locked the S2C and CLM evaluation demo for 4 Aug 2026, confirming the slot the day after it was proposed on 26 Jun 2026.
There is no rival vendor; the deal is a race against do-nothing inertia at a greenfield account that reopened procurement only about a year ago and runs entirely on manual ERP.
The deal is still single-threaded on Dwayne Nicolay, a Procurement Manager with influence but no budget authority, and no budget owner has been engaged.
A $200k multi-product build has no quantified ROI case and no confirmed budget owner, so urgency and price justification are unproven.

scope_change.from: iContract, iSource, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake (S2C + CLM + Merlin Intake)
scope_change.to: iContract, iSource, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake (S2C + CLM + Merlin Intake)
scope_change.detail: Scope unchanged since creation on 17 Jun 2026; full S2C plus CLM plus Merlin Intake remains in play with no modules dropped or amount cut.

customer_preference.evidence: Dwayne Nicolay engaged substantively in discovery and confirmed the evaluation demo, but has not stated a Zycus preference or asked for a proposal; positive interest without a decisive lean (25-26 Jun 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: 'completely running on manual ERP' with 'no proper structure', CLM and S2C prioritised, but no cost, cycle-time, savings, or ROI figure quantified (SF Next_Step__c; 25 Jun 2026 discovery).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A qualified greenfield $200k S2C+CLM+Merlin deal with a confirmed 4 Aug demo and an engaged but junior single contact; the win hinges on getting a budget owner in the room and quantifying the cost of doing nothing, not on beating a rival.

win_position.text: This sits mid-pack: real, confirmed pain (manual ERP, no structure) and a locked 4 Aug 2026 demo make it live, but it is only Qualified with a single Influencer and no proven preference, so it is early promise rather than a leading position.
No economic buyer is mapped for the $200k spend: only Dwayne Nicolay (Procurement Manager, Influencer) is engaged, and approval authority above him is unengaged (SF OpportunityContactRole).
There is no forcing event and no quantified ROI, so do-nothing inertia at a function reopened only ~1 year ago is the most likely way this stalls (25 Jun 2026 discovery).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is steady: Dwayne Nicolay confirmed the 4 Aug 2026 evaluation demo one day after it was proposed (26 Jun 2026), and the deck was already delivered.
Engagement is still rep-initiated and a generic-to-tailored demo away from qualification: no senior stakeholder has joined and no commercial topic has entered (SF activities).

customer_commitment.text: Dwayne Nicolay committed his time to a 90-minute evaluation demo and responded within a day, showing genuine intent to evaluate (26 Jun 2026).
Commitment is shallow: no budget owner brought in, no timeline for selection stated, and no Zycus-specific next step (proposal, POC) requested.

deal_risk.text: Single-thread risk: the entire deal rests on one Influencer with no budget authority, so a silent senior stakeholder could deprioritise it without warning (SF OpportunityContactRole).
Urgency risk: with no compelling event and an unquantified business case, a $200k greenfield spend is easy to defer against competing priorities (25 Jun 2026 discovery).

customer_preference.evidence: Engaged and curious but no stated Zycus lean or proposal request (25-26 Jun 2026).

economic_buyer.evidence: No budget owner mapped; only an Influencer engaged for a $200k spend.

urgency.evidence: No forcing event; do-nothing inertia at a greenfield account is the main threat.

expansion_context.note: New-business greenfield opportunity (SF Type = New Business, created 17 Jun 2026); no prior Closed-Won on this account, so executive and budget access must be earned, not inherited.

items.action: Send Dwayne a tailored 4 Aug demo agenda mapping CLM and S2C to his priorities.
items.trigger: Demo confirmed for 4 Aug 2026; buyer prioritised CLM and S2C over manual ERP
items.expected_effect: Sharpens the demo to Cox's stated priorities and increases conversion from evaluation to selection.

items.action: Ask Dwayne to bring the procurement budget owner to the 4 Aug demo.
items.trigger: Only Dwayne Nicolay (Influencer) engaged; no budget owner mapped for the $200k spend
items.expected_effect: Maps the economic buyer and breaks single-threading before the pivotal demo, protecting the Dec close.

items.action: Build a quantified cost-of-manual-process and ROI case for the 4 Aug demo.
items.trigger: Pain confirmed but unquantified; no forcing event against do-nothing inertia
items.expected_effect: Creates urgency and justifies the $200k spend to senior approval, countering status-quo inertia.

commentary.win_position: Win 16 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): generic demo only (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 8 is anchored on the stage win (16), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: A qualified greenfield $200k S2C+CLM+Merlin deal with a confirmed 4 Aug demo and an engaged but junior single contact; the win hinges on getting a budget owner in the room and quantifying the cost of doing nothing, not on beating a rival
